title: Avoid Overestimating the Present: - Zoom Out: Ask, “will This Matter in a Year
subtitle: Balance Present Significance
description:
To avoid overestimating the present:
- Zoom out: Ask, “Will this matter in a year? Five years?”
- Review the past: Compare this moment to similar past events for perspective.
- Plan ahead: Focus on long-term goals, not just immediate importance.

Example: Instead of stressing over a minor setback, think about how it fits into your bigger picture.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify how often you currently react impulsively to situations. Track the number of reactive messages sent and emotional responses over a week to establish a baseline.

howTo.title: Create the perspective check template
howTo.summary: Set up a micro-template in the Brali LifeOS app titled 'Perspective check'. Include fields for what happened, why it feels urgent, and whether it will matter in a year.

howTo.title: Implement daily perspective checks
howTo.summary: Commit to performing 1 to 3 perspective checks each day using the template. Reflect on the urgency of each situation and adjust your responses accordingly.

howTo.title: Review and adjust your approach
howTo.summary: At the end of each week, review your perspective checks. Assess which situations truly mattered and adjust your future responses based on these insights.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and ensure ongoing practice. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/present-bias-check/.
